title: Signal Tanker Weekly Report
summary:
Chart of the Week: VLCC Tonne Miles from West Africa & AG to China
VLCC tonne-miles from AG and West Africa to China exhibiting noticeable rise since May
Data Source: https://go.signalocean.com/e/983831/timeseries-tanker-downloadable/2p4z2v/331262881?h=oOcNwg5Pn8352_yq_oSs4JkIT3zDFJitCWYC1OYVRAc
The first week of July ended with rates in the crude oil freight market coming under pressure, while weaker sentiment in clean tanker freight rates continued. Interestingly, Chinese oil demand is driving up tonne-mile volumes for VLCC routes between the Arabian Gulf (AG) and West Africa despite signs of a downward correction. VLCC tonne-miles from Wafr and AG to China have increased significantly in July since early May.

children.title: Chart of the Week: VLCC Tonne Miles from West Africa & AG to China
section_path: Chart of the Week: VLCC Tonne Miles from West Africa & AG to China
children.section_path_text: Chart of the Week: VLCC Tonne Miles from West Africa & AG to China
children.summary:
VLCC tonne-miles from AG and West Africa to China exhibiting noticeable rise since May
Image reference: unnamed - 2023-07-10T125425.491.png | assets/2023-07-10_v437ovio1v3vh8uqmk11rdyp5x6fur_img_unnamed-2023-07-10t125425491_e74f709367ff.png
Data Source: https://go.signalocean.com/e/983831/timeseries-tanker-downloadable/2p4z2v/331262881?h=oOcNwg5Pn8352_yq_oSs4JkIT3zDFJitCWYC1OYVRAc
The first week of July ended with rates in the crude oil freight market coming under pressure, while weaker sentiment in clean tanker freight rates continued. Interestingly, Chinese oil demand is driving up tonne-mile volumes for VLCC routes between the Arabian Gulf (AG) and West Africa despite signs of a downward correction.

children.title: ‘Dirty' WS VLCC - Suezmax - Aframax Weaker
children.summary:
Image reference: unnamed - 2023-07-10T125626.189.png | assets/2023-07-10_v437ovio1v3vh8uqmk11rdyp5x6fur_img_unnamed-2023-07-10t125626189_f6167623f928.png
The first week of July ends with a mixed picture of the dynamics as dirty oil freight rates are under pressure, however, the VLCC MEG/China route indicated signs for a slightly upward momentum. VLCC MEG-China freight rates reached a level of 58 WS, up 3 points from the previous week, while the gap remains wide from the recent peak in week 24, when rates were at 80 WS.

children.title: ‘Product' WS LR Weaker
children.summary:
Image reference: unnamed - 2023-07-10T125654.339.png | assets/2023-07-10_v437ovio1v3vh8uqmk11rdyp5x6fur_img_unnamed-2023-07-10t125654339_eb4384632ea8.png
LR2 AG freight rates, which showed an upward trend the previous week, finally fell to WS98, down 25% from the previous week.

children.title: Panamax Weaker
children.summary:
Image reference: unnamed - 2023-07-10T125837.524.png | assets/2023-07-10_v437ovio1v3vh8uqmk11rdyp5x6fur_img_unnamed-2023-07-10t125837524_eb4384632ea8.png
Panamax Carib-to-USG rates continued the previous week's downward trend with rates around WS250, down nearly 19% from seven weeks ago. Image reference: unnamed - 2023-07-10T125929.936.png | assets/2023-07-10_v437ovio1v3vh8uqmk11rdyp5x6fur_img_unnamed-2023-07-10t125929936_a66c25caf5d1.png
MR1 rates for the Baltic continent are now around 130WS, 18 points lower than the levels of week 25
MR2 rates for shipments from the continent to the U.S.

children.title: SECTION 2/ SUPPLY Supply Trend Lines for Key Load Areas
section_path: SECTION 2/ SUPPLY Supply Trend Lines for Key Load Areas
children.section_path_text: SECTION 2/ SUPPLY Supply Trend Lines for Key Load Areas
children.summary:
Image reference: unnamed - 2023-07-10T130134.520.png | assets/2023-07-10_v437ovio1v3vh8uqmk11rdyp5x6fur_img_unnamed-2023-07-10t130134520_064a150e99af.png
Crude oil tanker supply painted a mixed picture in the first few days of July, as despite signs of a decline in the first week, there were ultimately more vessel movements, with the number of vessels roughly in line with the annual average. VLCC Ras Tanura: The current number of ships is only 3 ships below the annual average and is about 70, which is 10 ships more than the first signs at the beginning of the week.

children.title: MR1 (#vessels) - Decreasing
children.summary:
Image reference: unnamed - 2023-07-10T130517.509.png | assets/2023-07-10_v437ovio1v3vh8uqmk11rdyp5x6fur_img_unnamed-2023-07-10t130517509_84e254c3038b.png
Clean LR2 AG Jubail: The current ship count currently stands at the increased level of approximately 18, reflecting an increase of 5 vessels above the annual average. Clean MR1 Algeria Skikda: The current ship count is now around 30 vessels, almost 10 vessels lower than the previous week and 20 vessels lower than the sudden peak recorded in week 25.

children.title: ‘Dirty' Tonne Days Mixed
children.summary:
Image reference: unnamed - 2023-07-10T130700.372.png | assets/2023-07-10_v437ovio1v3vh8uqmk11rdyp5x6fur_img_unnamed-2023-07-10t130700372_678aa39b1990.png
Dirty Tonne Days: Mixed dynamics are also evident in crude oil tanker demand trends, as the percentage increase in VLCC ton-miles now appears to be declining after a sustained increase in week 20 through the previous week. A downward trend can also be observed in the Aramax segment, while the downward trend in the Suezmax segment seems to have been overcome with a slight increase from the low in week 26.

children.title: ‘Clean' Tonne Days Panamax Increasing - MR Decreasing
section_path: ‘Clean' Tonne Days Panamax Increasing - MR Decreasing
children.section_path_text: ‘Clean' Tonne Days Panamax Increasing - MR Decreasing
children.summary:
Image reference: unnamed - 2023-07-10T130800.394.png | assets/2023-07-10_v437ovio1v3vh8uqmk11rdyp5x6fur_img_unnamed-2023-07-10t130800394_f7358795ad29.png
Panamax tonne days: The first week of July ended with a significantly higher percentage growth than the lows recorded seven weeks ago, while the recent trend seems now to continue for the coming days. Clean MR tonne days: The sentiment of demand tonne miles growth continues to be weaker with the percentage growth persistently hovering at much lower levels than the peak recorded three weeks ago.
